profile.title: UX/Product Designer focused on strategy, impact and experience
profile.bio: I am a UX/Product Designer with strong expertise in designing digital products focused on user experience, conversion and business impact. With a background in Digital Marketing, SEO and AI, I integrate strategy, design and usability in continuous improvement and innovation processes. I have worked in academic contexts, B2B e-commerce and institutional communication, always guided by data, empathy and product vision.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Selection of UX/Product Design projects focused on strategy, usability and business impact.

fgvLaw.title: FGV LAW
fgvLaw.overview: Restructuring of the FGV LAW legal courses area with focus on usability and information organization. The project aimed to increase the visibility of offerings and facilitate navigation between options.
fgvLaw.discovery: I identified that users faced difficulty in locating and comparing courses. The current structure generated confusion and dispersion. Through interviews and heuristic analysis, I realized that the excess content was not helping in decision-making, but rather hindering it.
fgvLaw.solution: I designed a new panel with a tab system and thematic filters, reorganizing the visual and informational hierarchy. Options were grouped based on user objectives (legal area, course type, workload).
fgvLaw.iteration: After user testing, we simplified filter terminology and adjusted the order of information based on direct feedback. I refined the layout for quick reading and mobile adaptation.
outcomes: Significant increase in course visibility and interaction
Improvement in conversion rate from access to enrollments or leads
Reduction in navigation time to course selection
fgvLaw.insights: Navigation structure needs to guide, not just show. Clarity and relevant grouping directly influence the perception of value of a course.

direitoGV.title: Direito GV
direitoGV.overview: Reorganization of the Direito GV research area to improve visibility of academic projects and facilitate access to information about researchers and their lines of work.
direitoGV.discovery: The research area was fragmented and poorly accessible. Researchers had difficulty promoting their work and external users could not find relevant information about ongoing projects.
direitoGV.solution: I developed a new information architecture with categorization by thematic areas, researcher profiles and project timeline. I also created an advanced search system.
direitoGV.iteration: We conducted tests with students, professors and researchers. Navigation was adjusted based on feedback about nomenclature and priority order. I validated each change with involved stakeholders.
outcomes: Reduction in navigation time to find specific projects or topics
Increase in visits to researcher pages
direitoGV.insights: Institutional areas gain relevance when they are navigable, updated and strategically reflected in information architecture.

taliparts.title: Taliparts
taliparts.overview: Taliparts digital structuring and validation project, focused on publishing automotive parts on Mercado Livre, with integration to physical operations. The goal was to learn quickly, at low cost, about real demand, buyer behavior and competitive advantage.
taliparts.discovery: I conducted detailed benchmarking with automotive sector competitors. I interviewed mechanics and shopkeepers, modeled personas and applied the CSD Matrix to identify certainties, assumptions and doubts in the physical catalog.
taliparts.solution: I created a validation strategy with SEO for Mercado Livre, visual standardization of ads, categorization centered on buyer vocabulary and search history. I also organized KPIs and defined product prioritization plan.
taliparts.iteration: I tested products by thematic blocks, monitoring clicks, questions and conversion rate. I refined descriptions, titles and even item selection based on real performance.
outcomes: Sales growth with strategically prioritized products
Reduction of buyer doubts through description improvements
Creation of replicable process of publication + analysis + repositioning
taliparts.insights: Digital validation at low cost is possible — and necessary. Product logic needs to consider physical context, technical vocabulary and differentials perceived by the customer.

tvInstitucional.title: FGV Institutional TV
tvInstitucional.overview: Creation of a visual system for TVs in the FGV hall, with the objective of communicating events and institutional updates in an attractive, dynamic and accessible way to students and visitors.
tvInstitucional.discovery: Students ignored physical bulletin boards and institutional emails. I identified that the language of the channels was outdated and poorly integrated with the visual routine of the spaces.
tvInstitucional.solution: I implemented a digital panel with weekly content curation, focus on visual rhythm and immediate clarity of messages. The platform was designed to be automated, with flexibility for remote updates.
tvInstitucional.iteration: We tested types of animations, display time and contrast. We adjusted the visual calendar and optimized the layout based on feedback from students and coordination.
outcomes: Message retention in panels much superior to previous emails
Reuse of the solution by other school sectors
tvInstitucional.insights: Physical environments are also interfaces. When well designed, they inform, engage and connect — without needing login.

backlog.title: Strategic Backlog Cycle
backlog.description: Real challenges faced by stakeholders and UX solutions strategically applied to solve business problems.

items.challenge: FGV needed to increase visibility of courses, lectures and institutional updates.
items.solution: The solution was to implement a strategic digital panel in the institution's hall, with content curation and automated updates.
items.result: 72% increase in face-to-face engagement and greater institutional perception.
items.note: I understood that to communicate efficiently in physical environments, content needs to be thought of as a living interface: visual rhythm, contextual relevance and immediate clarity make all the difference in attention and message retention.

items.challenge: DIREITO FGV users had difficulty locating documents and relevant content within the academic portal.
items.solution: Redesign of the navigation experience focusing on features valued by the corporate audience and simplification of the quotation process.
items.result: 27% increase in quotation conversions and 40% reduction in purchase journey abandonment.
items.note: I realized that mapping user objectives has more value than following fixed institutional structures.

items.challenge: The FGV LAW course area had difficulty organizing content, making navigation difficult and impacting program visibility.
items.solution: I structured a new panel with segmented filters and tab system, reorganizing information hierarchy with focus on scannability and user decision journey.
items.result: There was a significant increase in course visibility and conversion of access to interactions with specific pages, plus greater clarity perceived by users.
items.note: I understood that when dealing with a large volume of options, information design needs to facilitate decision-making — not just show content, but organize choice with logic and context.

items.challenge: Taliparts needed to validate which products would be most competitive on Mercado Livre.
items.solution: I conducted detailed benchmarking with competition analysis, positioning and evaluations of similar products.
items.result: The initial product curation had higher viewing and interest rates in the first week of publication.
items.note: Analyzing the competitive terrain in depth is essential before any brand exposure in open channels.

items.challenge: There was a lack of clarity about who Taliparts' real target audience was.
items.solution: I modeled personas based on interviews with shopkeepers, mechanics and autonomous buyers.
items.result: We defined three strategic profiles that guided everything from product choice to communication tone.
items.note: Persona is more than an archetype — it's the lens through which the entire business is interpreted.

items.challenge: At Taliparts, the titles and descriptions of ads on Mercado Livre were not optimized for SEO on Mercado Livre.
items.solution: I restructured titles based on SEO practices specific to marketplaces and tested descriptions with frequent keywords.
items.result: Significant improvement in offer positioning and more than 20% increase in organic traffic in 6 months.
items.note: In the marketplace, SEO is as important as price — it's what brings the click.

items.challenge: At Taliparts it was necessary to validate whether the published products really had real demand.
items.solution: I structured a validation plan based on click metrics, buyer questions and benchmark comparison.
items.result: We refined the catalog based on performance, avoiding dead stock and redirecting efforts.
items.note: Testing small and adjusting quickly is more efficient than launching 100 products in the dark.

items.challenge: At FGV some pages had dense institutional content that kept users from reading in full.
items.solution: I applied UX writing techniques (scannability, objective titles, visual lists) to key pages.
items.result: Average reading time increased and there was a drop in page abandonment.
items.note: Small decisions in text have great impact on reading experience and comprehension.

contact.title: Let's Talk?
contact.description: I'm always open to new opportunities and collaborations. Get in touch with me through the form below.

form.name: Your name*
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell me about your project, opportunity or how I can help...
form.messageHint: Minimum 10 characters for a clear message
form.privacy: Your data will not be shared and will only be used to respond to your message.

errors.nameMinLength: Name must be at least 2 characters
errors.messageMinLength: Message must be at least 10 characters

success.title: Message sent!
success.description: Thank you for contacting me! I'll respond soon.

error.title: Error sending
error.description: Something went wrong. Try again or send directly to tbisp0@hotmail.com

feedback.typeQuestion: What type of feedback would you like to send?
feedback.problemInstruction: Describe the problem you encountered. Be as detailed as possible so we can understand and fix it quickly. Do not include sensitive personal information.
feedback.problemPlaceholder: Example: "The submit button is not working on the projects page."
feedback.ideaTitle: Share your idea to improve the site
feedback.ideaInstruction: Tell us your suggestion, feature or improvement you would like to see here. Your opinion is fundamental for us to evolve the site experience.
feedback.ideaPlaceholder: Example: "It would be interesting to have a category filter in projects."
feedback.praiseTitle: Leave your praise or positive comment
feedback.praiseInstruction: We're happy to know what you liked! Tell us what caught your attention or made a difference in your experience.
feedback.praisePlaceholder: Example: "I loved the design and navigation of the portfolio!"
feedback.defaultInstruction: Enter your comment, suggestion or question.
feedback.includeEmail: Include email to be contacted about your comments
feedback.importance: Your opinion is very important for the continuous improvement of the site.
